title: To M. Laurence Swetnaham.
content:
Read thou my lines, my Swetnaham; if there be
    A fault, 'tis hid if it be voic'd by thee.
    Thy mouth will make the sourest numbers please:
    How will it drop pure honey speaking these!
meaning:
"To M. Laurence Swetnaham." is a quintessential example of Robert Herrick's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
